summary: Customer Balance Manager is a full-stack Internet Computer (DFINITY) application with a Motoko canister backend and a React + TanStack React Query frontend. Authenticated users (via Internet Identity) can manage a customer ledger: create/update/delete customers, track outstanding balances and payment statuses, record payments (which updates balances and status), view aggregate stats (total customers, outstanding sum, counts by status), track a running 'total collected' amount with a reset option, bulk import customers from a CSV template, and generate/send WhatsApp payment reminder messages to customers. The backend also includes role-based access control initialization via a secret admin token and includes Caffeine blob-storage maintenance mixins for storage gateway authorization and dead-blob pruning.

existing_features.title: Internet Identity authentication provider
existing_features.description: Provides login/logout and persisted identity via `@dfinity/auth-client` wrapped in a React context (`InternetIdentityProvider`), including login status flags and 30-day delegation TTL configuration.

existing_features.title: Backend actor creation and per-identity actor caching
existing_features.description: Creates an anonymous or authenticated canister actor depending on identity, caches it in React Query by principal, and invalidates/refetches dependent queries when the actor changes.

existing_features.title: Access control initialization and RBAC utilities
existing_features.description: Motoko RBAC module supports admin/user/guest roles; initialization compares a user-provided secret to an env admin token to allow first-admin assignment. Includes endpoints to get caller role, check admin, and assign roles (admin-only). Frontend triggers initialization on actor creation using a secret read from URL hash/session.

existing_features.title: User profile storage and first-time profile setup
existing_features.description: Backend stores a per-principal `UserProfile {name}` with query/save endpoints. Frontend fetches the caller profile and, if missing, forces a profile setup modal to capture and persist the user's name before showing the dashboard.

existing_features.title: Customer CRUD (create/read/update/delete) with dialogs
existing_features.description: Backend supports creating, reading, updating, deleting individual customers and deleting all customers. Frontend provides dialog-based create/edit forms with validation and confirmation dialogs for deletes, plus React Query invalidation and toast feedback on mutations.

existing_features.title: Customer search and payment-status filtering
existing_features.description: Backend exposes search-by-term and filter-by-payment-status queries. Frontend currently performs client-side filtering in the dashboard UI and offers a status filter dropdown and search input.

existing_features.title: Outstanding balances aggregation and dashboard statistics
existing_features.description: Backend computes total outstanding balances across all customers. Frontend displays KPI cards (total customers, outstanding INR amount, counts by paid/partially paid/unpaid) and formats currency in INR.

existing_features.title: Payment collection and automatic status updates
existing_features.description: Backend records a payment against a customer, recalculates outstanding balance (floored at 0), and sets payment status to paid/partiallyPaid/unpaid based on the new balance. Frontend provides a payment collection dialog with balance preview and mutation-driven refresh.

existing_features.title: Total collected payments tracking and reset
existing_features.description: Backend maintains a running `totalCollectedPayments` accumulator updated on each recorded payment and exposes query/reset endpoints. Frontend displays the collected total in StatsCards and provides a confirmation dialog to reset it to zero.

existing_features.title: Bulk customer import from CSV with validation and template download
existing_features.description: Frontend provides a two-step import flow: upload/parse CSV, show validation results in a table, then import valid rows by creating customers (and attempting update on 'already exists'-style errors). Includes a downloadable CSV template and INR formatting in previews.

existing_features.title: Theming (dark/light) and dashboard shell
existing_features.description: App uses `next-themes` with Tailwind tokens for dark/light mode. Header includes branding, theme toggle, and user menu with logout; footer provides attribution. Layout switches between login screen, profile setup, and dashboard based on auth/profile state.

existing_features.title: Caffeine blob-storage maintenance mixin
existing_features.description: Backend includes a storage mixin that can update an authorized gateway principal list from a cashier canister, check authorization, list dead blobs for deletion, confirm/prune dead blobs and trigger GC, and allow the cashier principal to top up cycles for the canister.

existing_features.title: Canister upgrade migration wiring
existing_features.description: Backend uses Motoko's `with migration = Migration.run` pattern, with a migration module that copies customers, user profiles, payment records, and total collected payments across versions.

existing_features.title: WhatsApp payment reminder message dialog
existing_features.description: Frontend provides a WhatsApp message dialog that generates a payment reminder message for a selected customer (including balance/payment context) and opens a WhatsApp deep link (wa.me) to send the message. Includes helper utilities for message formatting/URL construction and integrates from the customer dashboard/table actions.

existing_features.title: Update the default WhatsApp reminder message template used by the "Send outstanding via WhatsApp" action so the message starts with a greeting and includes the selected customer’s name and formatted outstanding balance amount, using this exact template text (with placeholders replaced at runtime): "नमस्कार (Customer Name) आपली खाते बाकी लवकरात लवकर जमा करून सहकार्य करावे. (Outstanding Balance)".

new_feature_requests.summary: Allow configuring the default WhatsApp reminder message template (with placeholders like (Customer Name) and (Outstanding Balance)); set template text to: "नमस्कार (Customer Name) आपली खाते बाकी लवकरात लवकर जमा करून सहकार्य करावे. (Outstanding Balance)"

architectural_notes: Frontend uses TanStack React Query for all canister calls (queries/mutations) with cache invalidation on writes and toast notifications via `sonner`.
Frontend uses a dedicated `useActor` hook that creates a DFINITY actor based on the current Internet Identity and triggers access-control initialization on actor creation.
Authentication is handled by a custom React context/provider around `@dfinity/auth-client`, persisting identities across reloads and providing login/logout state machine.
UI composition follows a dashboard pattern (header/footer/main), with modal/dialog-based CRUD and confirmation flows; styling is Tailwind + shadcn/ui components + `next-themes` for dark/light mode.
Backend is a single Motoko actor that composes functionality via `include` mixins (authorization + blob-storage).
Authorization uses a small RBAC module (admin/user/guest) with first-admin assignment gated by an environment-provided admin token.
Data is stored in in-canister `Map` structures (customers keyed by name, profiles keyed by principal, payment records keyed by customer name) and a `totalCollectedPayments` accumulator.
Canister upgrade compatibility is managed via Motoko's migration pattern (`with migration = Migration.run`).
Backend exposes additional Caffeine blob-storage maintenance endpoints (authorized gateway list, dead-blob listing/pruning, cashier refill) controlled by env vars and principal authorization.
Repository now includes a reusable build-template (Dockerfile, deploy scripts, ICP YAML, workspace config) for building/deploying the React + Motoko canisters.
known_issues: Backend `PaymentRecord.timestamp` is hardcoded to `0` (no real timestamps recorded).
Backend `paymentRecords` is keyed by `customerName`, so only the most recent payment per customer is retained (no payment history list).
Backend `createCustomer` does not check for duplicates; adding the same name will overwrite (Map `add`) rather than reject—frontend import logic expects possible 'already exists' errors.
Backend `deleteAllCustomers()` clears only `customers` but leaves `paymentRecords` and `totalCollectedPayments` intact, which may conflict with the UI wording about removing payment history.
Backend `recordPayment` does not validate `amount > 0`; a negative amount could increase balance and decrease `totalCollectedPayments`.
Excel import UI advertises `.xlsx` but parsing is CSV-by-text only (`file.text()` + comma split), so real XLSX files will not import correctly.

`getSecretParameter()` reads secrets only from a simple hash fragment parsed as query params (e.g., `#caffeineAdminToken=...`); it will likely fail for hash-router style URLs like `#/route?caffeineAdminToken=...` despite helper comments implying broader support.
Blob storage env var key appears misspelled: `CAFFFEINE_STORAGE_CASHIER_PRINCIPAL` (triple 'F'); misconfiguration will trap at runtime.
Floating-point (`Float`) is used for balances/collected totals, which can introduce rounding/precision issues for currency.
